questionText: A curve $C$ has parametric equations $x = t^{3} - t$ and $y = 2t^{2}$. Find $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ in terms of $t$.

steps.description: Differentiate x with respect to t
steps.explanation: The curve is given parametrically, so we first find how $x$ changes as the parameter $t$ changes. This is written $\dot{x}$ or $\dfrac{dx}{dt}$.

steps.description: Differentiate y with respect to t
steps.explanation: In the same way, differentiate the formula for $y$ with respect to $t$ to obtain $\dot{y}$.
Because both $x$ and $y$ depend on $t$, the chain rule gives $\dfrac{dy}{dx} = \dfrac{dy/dt}{dx/dt}$, provided $\dot{x} \neq 0$.
Replace $\dot{y}$ and $\dot{x}$ with the expressions just found.
Cancel common factors where possible so the gradient is in its simplest form in terms of $t$.
This gives the slope of the tangent to the curve at any value of $t$ for which $\dot{x} \neq 0$.
